source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Noble Don Taylor Drillship SOBM Spill

context.region: Gulf of Mexico, Garden Banks Block 427

event.top_event: Loss of Containment - Subsea BOP Seal Failure
actions_taken: ROV deployed to inspect marine riser and subsea BOP
45 bbl pill spotted at measured depth of 2,970 feet
500-ft cement plug set in 11.75-inch by 11.875-inch casing
Cast iron bridge plug (CIBP) set at measured depth of 21,353 feet
Subsea BOP retrieved to surface for inspection
Upper VBR aft door removed and shipped to NOV for inspection and repair
Cartridge guard designed by NOV to protect cartridge and seal
event.summary: On 7 June 2017, the Noble Don Taylor drillship under contract to Shell reported an unauthorized discharge of approximately 94.2 barrels of 15.1 ppg synthetic oil base mud during exploration operations on Well DC005 (Cardamom) in Garden Banks Block 427, Gulf of Mexico. The spill was caused by failure of the upper variable bore ram (VBR) aft bonnet door cartridge seal on the subsea blowout preventer. Contributing factors included buildup of debris in the upper VBR body, failure to conduct proper hole cleaning and flushing of the BOP as recommended in NOV Product Information Bulletin released 11 August 2015, and inadequate communication of known risk of loss of seal integrity.
recommendations: Implement proper hole cleaning and flushing procedures for subsea BOP prior to operation
Improve communication of known risks identified in equipment manufacturer bulletins
Install cartridge guard to protect cartridge and seal from debris buildup and compression

hazards.name: Pressurized hydrocarbon and drilling fluid system

threats.name: Debris accumulation in subsea BOP upper VBR cavity
Inadequate hole cleaning and flushing prior to BOP operation
Cartridge seal degradation and compression damage

consequences.name: Unauthorized discharge of synthetic oil base mud to marine environment
Environmental pollution - 94.2 barrels of SOBM spilled
Equipment damage to subsea BOP upper VBR aft bonnet door

controls.name: Subsea blowout preventer (BOP) upper variable bore ram (VBR) cartridge seal assembly
controls.barrier_role: Primary containment barrier to prevent loss of drilling fluid
controls.lod_basis: Primary seal on subsea BOP to contain pressurized drilling fluid

supporting_text: the upper VBR aft door cartridge seal failed to seal
The failure of the cartridge seal was attributed to the buildup of debris that was compressed behind the upper VBR ram block cavity
BSEE investigation team visually observed a split in the aft door cartridge seal O-ring gasket and a small washed out area

controls.name: Hole cleaning and flushing procedure for subsea BOP
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to prevent debris accumulation in BOP
controls.lod_basis: Preventive maintenance procedure recommended by equipment manufacturer

supporting_text: failure to conduct proper hole cleaning and flushing of the BOP prior to operation as recommended in the NOV PIB released on 11 August 2015
NOV had released a PIB on 11 August 2015 that recommended proper hole cleaning and flushing of the subsea BOP prior to operating to avoid damage to the subsea BOP bonnet door cartridge seals

controls.name: NOV Product Information Bulletin (PIB) communication regarding BOP maintenance
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to communicate known risks and maintenance requirements
controls.lod_basis: Equipment manufacturer guidance on known risk of seal integrity loss

supporting_text: inadequate communications of a known risk of loss of seal integrity as stated in the 11 August 2015 NOV PIB

controls.name: Remote Operated Vehicle (ROV) inspection of marine riser and subsea BOP
controls.barrier_role: Detection and monitoring of subsea equipment condition
controls.lod_basis: Early detection of SOBM leak to enable response actions

supporting_text: On 7 June 2017 at 08:25 hr, the remote operated vehicle (ROV) was deployed to inspect the marine riser and subsea BOP
At approximately 09:00 hr, ROV personnel contacted rig management and reported that SOBM had been observed leaking from an area between the upper and middle pipe rams on the subsea lower triple BOP

controls.name: Cement plug installation and well abandonment procedures
controls.barrier_role: Containment and isolation of wellbore to stop fluid loss
controls.lod_basis: Regulatory-approved procedure to isolate well and prevent further discharge

supporting_text: At 15:40 hr, Shell received approval from BSEE to set a 500-ft cement plug in the 11.75-inch by 11.875-inch casing
At 07:22 hr, BSEE granted Shell approval to temporarily abandon the well
a cast iron bridge plug (CIBP) was set at a measured depth of 21,353 ft followed by a 500-ft cement plug

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
